ticket.title: Add --strict flag to `night-shift specify` that exits non-zero on any open question
ticket.description: Operators want CI-style enforcement: when `--strict` is passed, the specify CLI must fail (exit code != 0) if the agent surfaced any openQuestions. Default behavior unchanged.

specBundle.content:
# `--strict` flag for specify CLI

Add a new boolean flag `--strict` to `src/cli/specify.ts`. When set, after the specifier returns, exit with code 2 if `openQuestions.length > 0`. Without the flag, behavior is unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
- `night-shift specify --ticket X --strict` exits with code 2 if the response has any open questions.
- `night-shift specify --ticket X --strict` exits 0 when openQuestions is empty.
- `night-shift specify --ticket X` (no flag) exits 0 regardless of openQuestions, as today.
- `--help` lists the new flag.

- [ ] Parse `--strict` in `src/cli/specify.ts` argv handling
- [ ] After specify completes, branch on flag: exit 2 if openQuestions non-empty
- [ ] Update USAGE string to document the flag
- [ ] Add a unit test exercising both branches
